rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCHUGH et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Brayton Purcell LLP¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Andrew McHugh is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant General Electric Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DEL PINO VS. 3M COMPANY INDIVIDUALLY AND FKA MINNESOTA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 5, Defendant CBS Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DEL PINO VS. 3M COMPANY INDIVIDUALLY AND FKA MINNESOTA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 5, Defendant Eaton Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 5 and granted as to issue 6 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and adjudication issues 1 through 5, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Del Pino and Mr. Boness create a triable issue whether Mr. Del Pino was exposed to Cutler-Hammer asbestos-containing contactors and arc shields. As to summary adjudication of punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objection to plaintiffs¿ Exhibit K is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DEL PINO VS. 3M COMPANY INDIVIDUALLY AND FKA MINNESOTA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 5, Defendant Gould Electronics, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and all issues for summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to comprehensive contention discovery designed to elicit all evidence and information plaintiffs have to support their claims against it, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s inclusion with its moving papers only of responses to requests for production is insufficient. The Court did not consider the amended declaration of Khaled Taqi-Eddin or the exhibits attached thereto as they do not comply with the notice requirements set forth in C.C.P. 437c(a) and violate plaintiffs¿ due process rights. Plaintiffs¿ objections to defendant¿s Exhibit E, the purported responses to special interrogatories, are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL EDWARD FORT et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 16, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 6, Defendant Dixieline Lumber Co.¿s motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs¿ complaint pleads facts sufficient to state a claim for punitive damages and is denied upon such ground. Plaintiffs are granted 10 days leave to amend to properly specify upon which cause(s) of action they are stating a claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
